text:
WHOLE BODY PET/CT WITH 18F-FDG

SCAN TYPE: Whole-body positron emission tomography with computed tomography (PET/CT) using 18F-fluorodeoxyglucose (18F-FDG), acquired from vertex to mid-thighs. Low-dose, non-diagnostic CT acquired for attenuation correction and anatomic co-registration. IV contrast not administered for this study. Patient fasted for 6 hours prior to tracer injection. Serum glucose at time of injection: 94 mg/dL. Injected activity: 10.2 mCi (377 MBq) 18F-FDG. Uptake time: 62 minutes. Images reconstructed using time-of-flight iterative algorithm.

INDICATION: Marcus R. Ellison, 57-year-old male with BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic melanoma (Stage IV M1c), initially presenting with hepatic, axillary, cervical nodal, and extensive subcutaneous soft tissue involvement. Previously treated with Tafinlar/tram (first line, approximately 8 months, discontinued due to acquired resistance), followed by Yervoy/Opdivo combination immunotherapy. ipi permanently discontinued after cycle 3 secondary to grade 3 immune-mediated colitis. Patient subsequently resumed Opdivo 480 mg IV every 4 weeks as maintenance monotherapy. Most recent CT restaging approximately 3 months prior demonstrated deepening partial response with near-complete resolution of hepatic lesions and marked reduction in right axillary soft tissue, residual right axillary focus 0.9 cm, residual hepatic segment VII focus 0.6 cm. Current study obtained approximately 12 months after immunotherapy initiation for metabolic response assessment and disease surveillance.

COMPARISON: Prior PET/CT performed at initial staging (approximately 17 months prior). Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (approximately 3 months prior). Comparison made to both studies as clinically relevant.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
No hypermetabolic cervical, submandibular, parotid, or supraclavicular lymph nodes identified. Previously noted bilateral cervical level II/III adenopathy with SUVmax values of 6.8-9.2 on baseline PET/CT has completely resolved, with bilateral cervical chain now demonstrating only normal-caliber, non-FDG-avid nodes with background-level activity. There is normal physiologic FDG uptake in the oropharyngeal mucosa, salivary glands, and extraocular muscles. No hypermetabolic soft tissue foci in the scalp or face. The thyroid gland demonstrates mildly increased but diffusely heterogeneous FDG uptake bilaterally (SUVmax approximately 3.2), somewhat greater than expected for background, in keeping with patient's known history of immune-mediated thyroiditis on levothyroxine replacement — this pattern is a recognized finding in autoimmune thyroiditis and is not suggestive of malignant involvement. No discrete hypermetabolic thyroid nodule is identified. No intracranial FDG-avid foci. Brain metabolic activity is symmetric and physiologic throughout. No leptomeningeal or skull base foci of concern.

CHEST / AXILLAE / THORAX:
Right axilla: A small residual soft tissue density is identified in the right axillary region, measuring approximately 0.8 cm in short axis on the co-registered low-dose CT component, corresponding to the previously measured 0.9 cm focus on the diagnostic CT three months prior. The metabolic activity overlying this structure is markedly diminished compared to the baseline PET/CT at which time the right axillary conglomerate nodal mass demonstrated an SUVmax of 18.7. At the current examination, the focal FDG activity corresponding to this residual soft tissue measures SUVmax 2.1, which is only minimally above mediastinal blood pool reference activity (mediastinal blood pool SUVmax approximately 1.7 on this study). This activity level is well below any threshold interpretable as metabolically active malignancy and is most consistent with post-treatment fibrotic or necrotic residual tissue. No satellite or adjacent hypermetabolic foci are identified in the right axilla.

Left axilla: No pathologically enlarged or FDG-avid lymph nodes. Normal small nodes with background-level activity.

Mediastinum and hila: No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. No FDG-avid mediastinal foci. Normal physiologic great vessel activity.

Lungs: The lung parenchyma is clear. No pulmonary nodules, consolidations, or ground-glass opacities identified on the CT component. No pleural effusions. No hypermetabolic pulmonary foci. Normal physiologic activity at the cardiac base and diaphragmatic leaflets.

Pericardium and heart: Normal physiologic myocardial FDG uptake pattern. No pericardial effusion.

Breast / anterior chest wall: Several well-healed dermal/subcutaneous changes are noted in the anterior chest wall and upper back region on CT, consistent with prior wide local excision site (right forearm also shows small post-surgical change). No hypermetabolic subcutaneous or cutaneous foci anywhere along the trunk, chest wall, or thoracic back. Complete metabolic resolution of the previously documented multiple hypermetabolic subcutaneous nodules on the trunk and bilateral upper extremities (at least 9 discrete lesions with baseline SUVmax range 4.1-8.9), none of which show residual FDG-avid activity on the current examination.

UPPER BACK / PRIMARY SITE:
The prior primary melanoma site on the upper back demonstrates post-excisional changes and dermal thickening consistent with the prior surgical scar. No hypermetabolic foci at or adjacent to the primary site. Previously documented SUVmax of 12.4 at the primary skin lesion site is no longer present. Background dermal activity only.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. On the co-registered CT, the previously identified six discrete hypodense hepatic lesions are no longer appreciable as distinct lesions. The hepatic segment VII region, where the dominant hepatic metastasis originally measured 3.1 cm with an SUVmax of 14.3, now shows a faint area of subtle low-level FDG activity measuring SUVmax 1.8 — this is at or near background blood pool activity and does not meet any criterion for residual metabolically active disease. No discrete hepatic lesion is visible on CT at this location. The remainder of the liver parenchyma demonstrates uniform background-level FDG uptake without any focal photopenic or hypermetabolic defects. The segment II, IV, and VI hepatic regions, each previously involved by metastatic disease, show complete resolution of any focal lesion or FDG-avid activity.

Gallbladder, biliary system: Unremarkable. Normal physiologic activity.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. Physiologic splenic FDG uptake without focal abnormality.

Pancreas: Normal morphology and metabolic activity. No pancreatic mass or focal FDG-avid lesion.

Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands appear normal in morphology and size on CT. No focal adrenal FDG uptake above background.

Kidneys and collecting systems: Normal renal cortical FDG uptake bilaterally. Physiologic tracer excretion in collecting systems and ureters. No focal renal lesion.

Stomach and bowel: Mild, diffuse, heterogeneous bowel FDG uptake is present, predominantly in the left colon, consistent with patient's known history of prior grade 3 immune-mediated colitis and subsequent treatment. This pattern, with mild uptake along the descending colon to splenic flexure, is non-focal and is interpreted as most likely representing residual mucosal inflammatory changes or physiologic activity in the setting of prior colitis with documented endoscopic healing. No discrete focal FDG uptake in the colon to suggest active colitis or malignancy. No hypermetabolic pericolic or mesenteric nodes.

Peritoneum and retroperitoneum: No ascites. No peritoneal or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. No hypermetabolic retroperitoneal nodal disease. Previously noted right external iliac lymph node (1.2 cm at progression; 0.4 cm on last CT) is no longer discretely measurable and shows no FDG-avid activity.

Abdominal wall and subcutaneous tissues: No hypermetabolic subcutaneous or soft tissue lesions in the abdomen or flank. Complete metabolic resolution of previously documented abdominal wall subcutaneous nodules.

PELVIS:
No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No FDG-avid pelvic nodes. Normal bladder with physiologic tracer activity. Prostate appears unremarkable. No bony pelvic lesion.

MUSCULOSKELETAL / BONE MARROW:
No focal hypermetabolic skeletal or bone marrow lesions. Normal axial and appendicular skeletal activity. No lytic or sclerotic osseous changes on CT component to suggest bony metastatic disease. Background marrow activity is uniform and physiologic.

EXTREMITIES:
Right forearm demonstrates a small area of post-surgical soft tissue change consistent with prior wide local excision site for the previously resected cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (BRAF inhibitor-related adverse event). No FDG-avid activity at this site beyond physiologic wound/scar tissue signal (SUVmax 2.0, non-specific). No other hypermetabolic lesions in the upper or lower extremities. No focal subcutaneous or soft tissue nodules.

IMPRESSION:

1. Near-complete metabolic response to immunotherapy in the setting of metastatic BRAF V600E-mutant melanoma. This study represents a dramatic interval improvement compared to the initial staging PET/CT performed approximately 17 months prior, at which time widespread metabolically active disease was identified across hepatic, bilateral axillary, bilateral cervical, and multiple subcutaneous sites.

2. Residual small right axillary soft tissue focus (approximately 0.8 cm, SUVmax 2.1) demonstrates activity that is at or only minimally above mediastinal blood pool reference, far below any threshold for metabolically active disease. This finding is most consistent with post-treatment fibrous or necrotic residual tissue rather than viable tumor. No satellite hypermetabolic activity.

3. Subtle low-level FDG activity in the hepatic segment VII region (SUVmax 1.8, at background level) without a corresponding discrete CT-detectable lesion is not interpretable as residual active hepatic metastatic disease. This is most consistent with post-treatment parenchymal change.

4. Complete metabolic resolution of previously documented: bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy, multiple hepatic metastases (segments II, IV, VI), multiple trunk and extremity subcutaneous nodules, and right external iliac lymph node.

5. Mildly heterogeneous diffuse thyroid FDG uptake is compatible with the patient's known immune-mediated thyroiditis; no suspicious thyroid nodule.

6. Mild heterogeneous left colonic FDG activity is most likely physiologic or reflective of residual post-inflammatory mucosal changes given prior grade 3 immune-mediated colitis; no focal hypermetabolic colonic lesion to suggest active inflammation or neoplasia.

7. No new sites of hypermetabolic disease anywhere in the imaged field.

8. No FDG-avid intracranial lesions identified (note: brain MRI remains the preferred modality for intracranial surveillance in this patient).

Overall, the findings are consistent with an exceptional and durable response to checkpoint inhibitor immunotherapy, with near-complete metabolic remission of previously extensive stage IV disease.
